risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The allocation of the participants to the study arms was done centrally by block randomization (block size of 6) stratified by country... The Pharmacy Department of the Hospital Clinic in Barcelona produced and safeguarded the computer-generated randomization list for each recruiting site until unblinding..." Comment: The paper explicitly states that a computer-generated randomization list was used, with central block randomization stratified by country. This is a clearly random sequence generation method.

Quote: "The Pharmacy Department of the Hospital Clinic in Barcelona produced and safeguarded the computer-generated randomization list for each recruiting site until unblinding... Study number allocation for each participant was concealed in opaque sealed envelopes that were sequentially numbered and opened only after recruitment by study health personnel." Comment: This explicitly describes a concealment mechanism meeting SNOSE criteria (sequentially numbered, opaque, sealed envelopes) and states envelopes were opened only after recruitment, so the person enrolling participants could not foresee the upcoming assignment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "An individually randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial was conducted..." and "CTX plus IPTp-placebo (identical to MQ tablets in shape and colour)... Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study." Comment: The report explicitly states that participants and care providers/personnel were blinded, with matching placebo tablets used to maintain masking. Although adverse effects differed between groups, the paper does not state that blinding was broken. Therefore, based on explicit successful blinding procedures, performance bias is judged low overall.

Quote: "Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study." Quote: "An individually randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial was conducted..." Comment: Outcome assessors included laboratory staff/clinicians for malaria smears, PCR, histology, haemoglobin, viral load, infant HIV PCR, and birth outcomes. Although some outcomes involved participant report (e.g. adherence, some adverse-event symptoms), the report states participants were blinded, so self-reported and clinician-elicited outcomes were also assessed without knowledge of allocation. The main reported outcomes are largely objective or standardized, and assessor blinding was explicitly stated; therefore detection bias is low.

Quote: "Overall, 1,071 pregnant women were randomized to receive IPTp (537 were allocated to control and 534 to MQ)." "A total of 973 peripheral blood samples from women at delivery were collected (490 from control and 483 from MQ group)." "The main analysis was done on the Intention-to-Treat (ITT) cohort that included all recruited women that met the inclusion criteria and had data on the specific outcomes." Comment: For the primary outcome at delivery, analysed numbers are reported by arm: 490/537 control and 483/534 MQ, implying missing primary-outcome data for 47/537 (8.8%) and 51/534 (9.6%), an absolute imbalance of about 0.8 percentage points. This is low overall attrition, balanced between groups, at the analysed time point (delivery). Although detailed reasons for all missing primary-outcome data are not provided in the supplied text, the reported loss is under ~10% with minimal arm imbalance, which supports low risk of attrition bias for outcomes measured at delivery.
